refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable monitoring, controlling, and optimizing energy consumption to meet the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad; decomposing it into sub-functions clarifies how energy efficiency can be achieved through measurement, control, and optimization.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify by measuring energy_per_ton over the evaluation period and confirming it meets ≤ E_target; validate monitoring data accuracy, control actions, and optimization effectiveness.
sub_frs: FR1: Measure energy consumption and tonnage moved accurately.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage.
FR3: Optimize operational parameters to improve energy efficiency.

candidate_dps: DP1: Integrate energy and load sensors with data acquisition system.
DP2: Implement vehicle control algorithms for energy-efficient operation.
DP3: Develop adaptive optimization software for operational parameters.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Integrate energy and load sensors with data acquisition system.

potential_sources: Data dependencies between measurement and control modules
Communication latency between sensors and control algorithms
coupling_check.notes: DP1 is foundational and logically decoupled from control and optimization DPs; interfaces are well-defined.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate sensor calibration and reliable data acquisition.
Risk of sensor failure impacting energy measurement accuracy.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider modular sensor interface abstraction to mitigate sensor failure risk.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical components including energy and load sensors and onboard data acquisition hardware.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Transmit energy consumption and tonnage data to NGHE Solution for monitoring and optimization.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication for sensor data and control commands.

module_containment_scores.notes: Sensors and data acquisition are physically contained within Vehicle; minimal diffusion.
Receives data but does not contain sensors; higher diffusion due to data sharing.
Limited direct involvement; mostly data consumer.
No containment; only indirect data usage.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy measurement DP is best contained within the Vehicle module to ensure data integrity and reduce interface complexity.
containment_risks: Potential integration complexity with vehicle hardware.
Sensor maintenance and calibration responsibility concentrated in Vehicle.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and maintenance procedures between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR sub-function of measuring energy consumption accurately.

diffusion_risks: Data sharing across modules may cause latency or synchronization issues.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment within Vehicle module is strong, minimizing diffusion risks.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by testing sensor data accuracy and telemetry transmission.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of measurement DP to Vehicle IM with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Formalize interface specifications for Telemetry and Network Link.

decision.justification: The current level decomposition and assignments are sound, but further decomposition of control and optimization FRs is needed to fully cover the energy efficiency KPI.
next_level_sub_frs: FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage.
FR3: Optimize operational parameters to improve energy efficiency.

revise.why: To decompose remaining sub-functions for control and optimization to complete the functional coverage.

assumptions: Sensor data is reliable and timely.
Vehicle module can support additional control and optimization functions.
open_risks: Potential latency in telemetry affecting real-time optimization.
